identity.canonical_title: TIDAL WAVELENGTH QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula: λ_tidal

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 1147] TIDAL WAVELENGTH QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula: $\lambda_{tidal} = \oint_{S^1} k \cdot dl = \frac{2\pi R_{\oplus}}{n} \approx 20,015$ km Formal Technical Dissertation: Theorem 1. Demonstrates that the tidal field wavelength is not arbitrary but is enforced by topological boundary conditions on the Earth's closed manifold ($S^2$), requiring exactly $n=2$ wavelengths to satisfy phase matching. Source:
theory.technical_interpretation: TIDAL WAVELENGTH QUANTIZATION Canonical Statutory Formula: λ_tidal is a contour integral, written λ_tidal = oint_S^1 k · dl = frac2π R_oplusn ≈ 20,015. It is evaluated by integrating along a closed contour, capturing global or topological properties of the field. It is built from π, S, k, l, R_, n, defining λ_tidal. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
